steps.title: Use the near-zero evidence
steps.content: The sentence contrasts strong effects on individual consumers with national-level effects. The example says recent oil shocks had an effect on China's GDP that was only slightly greater than zero, so the blank needs a word meaning muted or small in force.

steps.title: Reject unsupported qualities
steps.content: B means inconsistent or changing, but the example is about size, not variability. C means helpful, which the near-zero GDP effect does not show. D means lasting over time, but the evidence does not discuss duration. The answer is A.
